title: Mildly Spicy Jamaican Lamb Tagine
description: A nutty and velvety Jamaican dish featuring lamb mince, tempered with anti-inflammatory friendly spices, cooked in a ceramic tagine for a quick and flavorful meal.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in the ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink.
Add the chopped scotch bonnet pepper, curry powder, turmeric, and cumin, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the lamb is tender.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro, if desired.
